sorting: Bubble Sort - Repeatedly compares adjacent elements, O(n²) time, simple but slow, good for learning fundamentals
Merge Sort - Divide-and-conquer approach, O(n log n) time, stable sort preserving equal element order
Quick Sort - Partition-based sorting, O(n log n) average time, very efficient in practice
Selection Sort - Find minimum and swap, O(n²) time, few writes making it good for slow memory
Insertion Sort - Build sorted array incrementally, O(n²) time, efficient for small arrays
searching: Linear Search - Check each element sequentially, O(n) time, works on unsorted arrays
Binary Search - Eliminate half remaining elements each step, O(log n) time, requires sorted array
Jump Search - Jump by fixed size then linear search, O(√n) time, good middle ground
Hash-based Lookup - O(1) average time using hash tables, very fast but needs hash function
graph_algorithms: Breadth-First Search (BFS) - Explore all neighbors before going deeper, finds shortest path in unweighted graphs
Depth-First Search (DFS) - Go deep before backtracking, uses less memory, useful for topological sorting
Dijkstra's Algorithm - Find shortest path with weighted edges, greedy approach with priority queue
Floyd-Warshall - Find shortest paths between all pairs, O(n³) time, good for small dense graphs
dynamic_programming: Fibonacci - Compute series by building up from base cases, shows memoization benefits
0/1 Knapsack - Select items with weight constraint maximizing value, classic DP problem
Longest Common Subsequence - Find longest sequence common to two strings, string comparison
Edit Distance - Minimum operations to transform one string to another, spell correction
greedy_algorithms: Activity Selection - Select maximum non-overlapping activities, greedy choice of earliest end time
Huffman Coding - Build optimal prefix-free code tree, data compression technique
Kruskal's Algorithm - Build minimum spanning tree selecting lightest edges, greedy choice property
complexity_analysis: Big O Notation - Describes upper bound growth rate: O(1) constant, O(n) linear, O(n²) quadratic
Time vs Space Tradeoff - Some algorithms use more memory for faster time or vice versa
Best, Average, Worst Case - Different inputs cause different performance, quicksort worst case O(n²)
Amortized Analysis - Average cost per operation over sequence, important for data structures with occasional expensive ops
